evals.prompt: I just added a new authentication module to the API package in our monorepo. The files are packages/api/src/auth/middleware.ts (new, 85 lines) and packages/api/src/auth/types.ts (new, 22 lines). I also updated packages/api/src/index.ts to register the auth routes (3 lines added). Can you write me a commit message?
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'feat', scope 'api', imperative mood subject describing the new auth module, and optionally a body with more detail.
assertions: The commit message starts with 'feat(api):' because new source files were added to the api package
The subject line uses imperative mood (e.g., 'add' not 'added' or 'adds')
The subject line starts with a lowercase letter after the colon and space
The subject line does not end with a period
The total first line (type + scope + subject) is under 72 characters
The commit message mentions authentication or auth in the subject or body

evals.prompt: I fixed a date parsing bug. Changed 2 lines in src/utils/date.ts — the month was off by one because of zero-indexed months in JavaScript. No other files changed. Write a commit message for this.
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'fix', no scope or scope 'utils', imperative mood subject about fixing the date parsing bug.
assertions: The commit message starts with 'fix' as the type prefix because this is a bug fix
The subject line uses imperative mood
The subject line starts with a lowercase letter after the colon and space
The subject line does not end with a period
The commit message references the off-by-one error or month calculation issue
The total first line is under 72 characters

evals.prompt: I updated the GitHub Actions workflow file at .github/workflows/ci.yml to add a new linting step, and I also updated .github/workflows/deploy.yml to use Node 20 instead of Node 18. Generate a commit message.
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'ci', no scope needed, describing the CI configuration updates.
assertions: The commit message uses 'ci' as the type prefix because only CI configuration files changed
The subject line uses imperative mood
The subject line starts with a lowercase letter after the colon and space
The subject line does not end with a period
The commit message mentions the CI/workflow changes (linting step or Node version update)
